data.title: Owens Corning beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Owens Corning press release (NYSE:OC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.22 beats by $0.31.
  • Revenue of $2.84B (+23.5% Y/Y) beats by $70M.
  • Produced Operating Cash Flow of $1.9 Billion and Free Cash Flow of $1.2 Billion.
  • Returned $638 Million, or 51%, of Free Cash Flow to Shareholders through Dividends and Share Repurchases.
  • Outlook:
  • For the first-quarter 2025, the company expects to continue delivering strong results, reflecting structural changes to the company and its cost structure, even in a mixed market environment. It expects revenue from continuing operations to grow mid-20 percent, compared to prior year’s revenue of $2.0 billion adjusted for glass reinforcements being moved to discontinued operations. The enterprise is expected to generate EBITDA margin from continuing operations of low-20 percent.

  • Current 2025 financial outlook is presented below.

    General Corporate Expenses

    $240 million to $260 million(1)

    Interest Expense

    $250 million to $260 million

    Effective Tax Rate on Adjusted Earnings

    24% to 26%

    Capital Additions

    Approximately $800 million(2)

    Depreciation and Amortization

    Approximately $650 million(3)

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Traders turn selective despite ongoing broad rally, grains stand out
data.description:
Following several weeks of widespread buying across most commodities and sectors, hedge funds began to exhibit signs of caution, adopting a more selective approach during the week leading up to February 18, Saxo Bank said in its weekly commitment of traders update.

This period saw the Bloomberg Commodity Index climbing by 1.6% to reach a new 17-month high, buoyed by gains across all sectors except livestock, the report said.

"The standout performers were the grains sector, bolstered by wheat (W_1:COM) and corn (C_1:COM), alongside the energy sector, where a strong 14% surge in natural gas prices (NG1:COM) more than compensated for losses in crude and fuel contracts."

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:WEAT), (SOYB), (NYSEARCA:CORN), (NYSEARCA:DBA), (NYSEARCA:MOO)

Almost non-stop buying since November has seen the natural gas net long jump to a four-year high as traders respond to an improved outlook for U.S. domestic demand and rising LNG exports, both potentially underpinning higher prices. However, in the short term, with many fresh longs in the market, the risk of a pullback has risen, Ole Hansen, Head of Commodity Strategy at Saxo Bank.

Meanwhile, crude oil and gold, two of the most popular contracts for trading, experienced net selling.

This was a reaction to the persistent weakness in crude oil prices and a gold market that required consolidation after an eight-week rally, which had pushed prices close to $3,000.

On the day, spot gold was trading +0.18% higher at $2,939.16 an ounce.

Looking at the agricultural sector, concerns about cold weather in the United States and Russia reportedly prompted short-covering across the Chicago and Kansas wheat contracts.

Despite this activity, a net short position was maintained in both, highlighting the need for even stronger gains to trigger a change in the overall negative sentiment, Hansen added.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Stock index futures advance as markets look to shake off Friday's sell-off
data.description:
Stock index futures climbed higher on Monday, aimed for a positive start after U.S. stocks ended last week on a gloomy note.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.6%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.4%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.7%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.23%.

U.S. stocks tumbled Friday, as investors closed the week with gloomy economic updates and word of a government investigation into UnitedHealth (UNH) in hand.

"Now recapping last week, which ended with a big risk-off move on Friday following more stagflationary U.S. data alongside some scares around the reporting of the new coronavirus discovered by researchers at the Wuhan Institute of Virology," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

"However, the earlier data started the softness…the overall backdrop weighed on risk assets, with the S&P 500 posting its worst day of 2025 so far, leaving it -1.66% lower on the week," Reid added.

Investors will keep their eyes on some big earnings releases this week. Home Depot (HD) and Lowe's Companies (LOW) will report on Tuesday and Wednesday, respectively.

The performance of the two companies will be under the radar as investors look to gauge the demand from U.S. consumers.

Nvidia (NVDA) will release its results on Wednesday.

On the economic side, the January Chicago Fed National Activity Index and the February Dallas Fed Manufacturing Survey are due today.

data.title: Sandoz launches biosimilar Pyzchiva in U.S. for all approved Stelara indications
data.description:
Sandoz (OTCQX:SDZNY) Monday said that Pyzchiva (ustekinumab-ttwe) is now commercially available to patients across the U.S.

Developed by Samsung Bioepis Co., and commercialized by Sandoz, Pyzchiva has been approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for the treatment of certain chronic inflammatory diseases, with the same indications as J&J's (JNJ) Stelara.

Pyzchiva is a key biosimilar value driver for Sandoz, contributing to the company’s overall growth strategy.

The commercial availability of Pyzchiva, which builds on the U.S. launch of Hyrimoz in July 2023, marks an important step in the Sandoz strategic ambition to become number one in biosimilars in the U.S., it said in a statement.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Monday: HE, EQX, and more
data.description:
Stock futures ticked up early Monday, bouncing back from last week’s losses, as market participants turned their focus to Nvidia’s (NVDA) highly anticipated earnings report.

Here are some of Monday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Equinox Gold (NYSE:EQX) shares gained 3% after the company announced plans to acquire all outstanding common shares of Calibre Mining (OTCQX:CXBMF) in a court-approved deal. Under the agreement, Calibre shareholders will receive 0.31 Equinox shares for each Calibre share held, with existing Equinox shareholders owning 65% of the combined company and former Calibre shareholders holding 35%. The merged entity is expected to have a market capitalization of C$7.7B and establish a diversified gold producer with operations across five countries, anchored by two high-quality, long-life, low-cost Canadian gold mines. With full ownership of Greenstone and Valentine, the new Equinox Gold will become Canada’s second-largest gold producer. The company is projected to produce around 950,000 ounces of gold in 2025, excluding output from Valentine and Los Filos.

Biggest stock losers

  • Hawaiian Electric (NYSE:HE) shares declined more than 5% after the company reported weaker-than-expected Q4 results. Revenue fell 6% year-over-year, while adjusted EPS of $0.20 missed estimates by $0.20. The utility's dividend to HEI remains suspended, with the holding company’s cash needs reduced following its recent equity issuance and the continued suspension of dividends to common shareholders.
  • Alibaba’s (NYSE:BABA) U.S.-listed shares fell 3% despite the company announcing a $52.44B investment in cloud computing and AI infrastructure over the next three years, exceeding its total spending in the sector over the past decade. The move comes amid intensifying global competition, with Alibaba reinforcing its commitment to cloud expansion during its earnings announcement. CEO Eddie Wu highlighted cloud computing as the company’s strongest AI revenue driver, with demand for AI hosting services surging. The company is also developing large language models (LLMs) to compete with global rivals and recently introduced its Qwen2.5-VL model, claiming it outperformed OpenAI’s GPT-4o, Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet, and Google’s Gemini 2.0 Flash in various AI evaluations.

data.title: Piraeus Financial GAAP EPS of €0.81, revenue of €2.8B; initiates guidance for 2025-2028
data.description:
• Piraeus Financial Holdings S.A. ADR press release (OTCPK:BPIRY): FY GAAP EPS of €0.81.
  • Revenue of €2.8B, up by 7% in 2024; fees grew 4x vs net interest income, rising by 16% y/y vs +4% y/y of NII, benefiting from strong growth of client balances.
  • Pro forma CET1 ratio stood at 14.7% and total capital ratio at 19.9%, both including 35% distribution accrual; MREL ratio reached 29.2% in Dec.24; Piraeus Pillar 2 Requirement was reduced by the ECB to 2.90% from 3.00% for 2025.

data.title: Eurozone annual inflation rate rises to 2.5% in January
data.description:
• The annual inflation rate in the Euro Area edged up to 2.5% in January from 2.4% in December, slightly above market expectations of 2.4%, a preliminary estimate showed.

  • Core consumer prices in the Euro Area increased 2.7% in January 2025 over the same month in the previous year.

  • It was the highest inflation rate since July 2024, driven primarily by a sharp acceleration in energy costs.

  • The Consumer Price Index in the Euro Area decreased 0.3% in January 2025 over the previous month.

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Bybit hack: $1.5B stolen from ethereum wallet in largest ever crypto heist
data.description:
Bybit said it successfully navigated the biggest cryptocurrency heist ever recorded, in which hackers stole about $1.5B in digital assets from the crypto exchange's ethereum cold wallet.

Hackers stole over 400,000 ether (ETH-USD) and staked ETH from the wallet. Customers immediately rushed to withdraw their assets from Bybit, with the exchange processing over 350,000 withdrawal requests within 12 hours of the hack.

"Bybit's 1:1 reserve guarantee ensured that client assets remained fully intact," the company said. Its CEO Ben Zhou reassured users that Bybit is solvent and can cover the loss, later disclosing that the exchange fully closed the ETH gap.

British blockchain analytics firm Elliptic, which traced the stolen crypto, said North Korea's Lazarus Group was behind the hack. Lookonchain, another analytics firm, said Bybit secured around 446,870 ETH (~$1.23B) since the hack through loans, whale deposits, and purchases.

The scale of the Bybit hack surpasses the $570M worth of Binance BNB (BNB-USD) stolen in 2022 and over $610M in crypto stolen from Poly Network in 2021.

"Despite this unprecedented breach, Bybit's swift and transparent crisis management ensured that the crypto market remained stable," the exchange said. It successfully froze $42.89M in assets through "rapid, coordinated action" among crypto firms, and major exchanges blacklisted the hacker's wallets.

Bybit worked closely with regulators and law enforcement agencies to address the hack. A comprehensive investigation is ongoing, and the company has pledged to implement additional preventive measures and strengthen its security infrastructure.

At the time of writing, ether (ETH-USD) traded 3.8% lower at $2,699, while bitcoin (BTC-USD) was down 0.8% at $95,626.

data.title: PostNL N.V. GAAP EPS of €0.096, revenue of €937M; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
